title: TC009-Publish a ride successfully with all required fields
description: Verifies a driver can publish a ride and sees the success snackbar.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Navigate to /login (explicit test step) by visiting http://localhost:5173/login.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/login")
        
        # -> Navigate to the alternate server URL http://localhost:5174/login and wait for the page to load so interactive elements can be inspected.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174/login")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/home' in current_url
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/publish' in current_url
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Ride publish ho gayi!')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "Expected 'Ride publish ho gayi!' to be visible"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- SPA at http://localhost:5174/login did not render any interactive elements (blank page), preventing the test from proceeding.
- Navigation to primary (http://localhost:5173) and fallback (http://localhost:5174/login) server URLs resulted in pages with 0 interactive elements, so login and publish actions cannot be performed.
- Required UI elements for login (email/username field, password field, 'Log in' button) were not present on the page.
- Required UI elements for publishing (main navigation 'Publish', publish form fields, 'Publish' button) were not present or reachable because the SPA did not load.
- No alternative navigation links or controls were available on the page to reach the target functionality.

title: TC013-Search rides with date and successfully book seats
description: Verifies a passenger can search by origin/destination/date and complete a booking with a visible confirmation.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5173
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173")
        
        # -> Navigate to /login by appending '/login' to the current site's base URL (http://localhost:5173/login).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5173/login")
        
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5174/login and wait for the SPA to load (up to 30s). If page remains blank (0 interactive elements), stop and report site unavailable.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5174/login")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/home' in current_url
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/search' in current_url
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST FAILURE

ASSERTIONS:
- SPA did not render on http://localhost:5174/login; page contains 0 interactive elements.
- Navigation to http://localhost:5173/login failed (site unavailable).
- After waiting a total of 30 seconds on http://localhost:5174/login, the page remained blank with 0 interactive elements.
- Login steps (email/password input and Login button) were not present, so authentication could not be performed.
- Booking/search flow could not be verified because the application UI did not load.
